provenance.upstream_generation_method: curated starter baseline with declared framings

evidence.title: Egg production has environmental impacts that vary by system and comparison
evidence.claim: An environmental reading of “good to eat” evaluates greenhouse-gas emissions, land use, feed production, manure, eutrophication, and other impacts; results depend on production system, functional unit, geography, and the food used as a comparator.
evidence.evidence_quality: Life-cycle assessment is the appropriate general method, but estimates are model-dependent. System boundaries, allocation choices, feed assumptions, geography, and the unit of comparison can materially change results.
evidence.strongest_counterargument: Comparative global averages can obscure local production practices and nutritional substitution. A per-kilogram, per-protein, per-calorie, and per-serving comparison can rank foods differently, so an environmental verdict without a declared functional unit is underspecified.
evidence.confidence_assessment: No numeric confidence is assigned. The robust conclusion is structural: environmental assessment requires a different source universe and outcome definition than either health or welfare assessment.

citations.citation: Poore, J., & Nemecek, T. (2018). Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers. *Science* 360(6392):987–992. https://doi.org/10.1126/science.aaq0216

Leinonen, I., Williams, A. G., Wiseman, J., Guy, J., & Kyriazakis, I. (2012). Predicting the environmental impacts of chicken systems in the United Kingdom through a life cycle assessment: Egg production systems. *Poultry Science* 91(1):26–40. https://doi.org/10.3382/ps.2011-01635

Abín, R., Laca, A., Laca, A., & Díaz, M. (2018). Environmental assessment of intensive egg production: A Spanish case study. *Journal of Cleaner Production* 179:160–168. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jclepro.2018.01.067

unattributed: The baseline assessment has not yet been independently audited.

notes: Baseline prose and scores are preserved as claims by the upstream run, not adopted as our judgments.
